title: Was The Last Supper Actually A PAGAN Ritual?
summary: This interview explores the hypothesis that Christ's Last Supper was derived from pagan mystery rituals, particularly Dionysian cult practices. It references Martin Luther King's essay on pagan influences on Christianity and scholar Dennis McDonald's comparisons between Christian communion and Dionysian flesh-eating and blood-drinking rituals.

key_claims.claim: Martin Luther King wrote an essay called 'The Influence of Mystery Religions on Christianity' positing the Pagan Continuity Hypothesis
The Last Supper's eating flesh and drinking blood points to Dionysian cult imagery
Dionysian initiates believed they gained immortality by eating the flesh and blood of the god
The cult of Dionysus involved two related rites: sparagmos and omophagia
